questions.questionText: [PLACEHOLDER] Your agentic system occasionally calls a tool with a malformed argument that passes schema validation but fails at runtime. What is the most robust way to handle this?

options.A: Wrap tool execution in a runtime validation layer that checks semantic constraints beyond the schema, and return structured errors the agent can react to.
options.B: Ask the user to rephrase their request until the tool call succeeds.
options.C: Silently retry the same tool call up to three times.
options.D: Disable the tool entirely until a developer manually reviews the logs.

questions.explanation: Schema validation only guarantees shape, not semantic correctness. A runtime validation layer catches constraint violations schemas can't express, and returning structured errors lets the agent self-correct instead of failing silently or looping.
